zh.lifeSummary: 當Alice Alicia Maud Bere於1394年出生在英國賀福爾時，她的父親Sir Kynard De La Bere38歲，母親Margaret Abrahall34歲。她於1415年與John Blount III在英國斯塔福爾結婚。他們至少是11個兒子和8個女兒的父母。她去世於1440年10月28日在英國英格蘭史洛普爾金萊特享年46歲。

nameMeanings.name: Bere
nameMeanings.attribution: Dictionary of Family Names in Britain and Ireland © University of the West of England 2016

variants.name: Beer
meanings: Some characteristic forenames:  German Kurt, Otto, Erhard, Hans, Heinz, Aloys, Bodo, Erna, Ernst, Frieda, Friedhelm, Hanni.

English (West Country): habitational name from any of the forty or so places in southwestern England called Beer(e) or Bear(e). Most of these derive their names from the West Saxon dative case, beara, of Old English bearu ‘grove, wood’ (the standard Old English dative bearwe being preserved in Barrow ). In some cases the surname may be topographic in origin, from atte beare ‘at the grove’. Some may be from Middle English bere ‘woodland swine-pasture’ (Old English bǣr). Compare Bear 1.

nameMeanings.name: Alice
nameMeanings.attribution: Dictionary of First Names © Patrick Hanks and Flavia Hodges 1990, 2003, 2006.
meanings: Originally a variant of Adelaide , representing an Old French spelling of a reduced form of Germanic Adalheidis. Alice and Adelaide were already regarded as distinct names in English during the medieval period. Alice enjoyed a surge of popularity in the 19th century and periods of favour ever since. It was the name of the central character of Lewis Carroll's Alice's Adventures in Wonderland ( 1865 ) and Through the Looking Glass ( 1872 ), who was based on his child friend Alice Liddell , daughter of the Dean of Christ Church, Oxford.

nameMeanings.name: Alicia
nameMeanings.attribution: Dictionary of First Names © Patrick Hanks and Flavia Hodges 1990, 2003, 2006.
meanings: Modern Latinate form of   Alice .  It was borne by the British ballerina  Alicia Markova  (born  Lilian Alicia Marks ,  1910–2004 ).

variants.name: Alisha
meanings: Modern Latinate form of   Alice .  It was borne by the British ballerina  Alicia Markova  (born  Lilian Alicia Marks ,  1910–2004 ).

variants.name: Alisia
meanings: Modern Latinate form of   Alice .  It was borne by the British ballerina  Alicia Markova  (born  Lilian Alicia Marks ,  1910–2004 ).

nameMeanings.name: Maud
nameMeanings.attribution: Dictionary of First Names © Patrick Hanks and Flavia Hodges 1990, 2003, 2006.
meanings: Medieval vernacular form of Matilda . This form was characteristically Low German (i.e. including medieval Dutch and Flemish). The wife of William the Conqueror , who bore this name, was the daughter of Baldwin , Count of Flanders. In Flemish and Dutch the letter -t- was generally lost when it occurred between vowels, giving forms such as Ma(h)auld. Maud or Matilda was also the name of the daughter ( 1102–67 ) of Henry I of England; she was married early in life to the Holy Roman Emperor Henry V , and later disputed the throne of England with her cousin Stephen . In 1128 she married Geoffrey , Count of Anjou. A medieval chronicler commented, ‘she was a good woman, but she had little bliss with him’. The name Maud became quite common in England in the 19th century, when its popularity was influenced in part by Tennyson's poem Maud, published in 1855 , but has not been much used since the early decades of the 20th century.

en.lifeSummary: When Alice Alicia Maud Bere was born in 1394, in Herefordshire, England, her father, Sir Kynard De La Bere, was 38 and her mother, Margaret Abrahall, was 34. She married John Blount III in 1415, in Staffordshire, England. They were the parents of at least 11 sons and 8 daughters. She died on 28 October 1440, in Kinlet, Shropshire, England, United Kingdom, at the age of 46.

ko.lifeSummary: Alice Alicia Maud Bere(이)가 1394년 영국 허퍼드실르에서 태어났을 때, 그녀의 아버지 Sir Kynard De La Bere(은)는 38세였고, 그녀의 어머니 Margaret Abrahall(은)는 34세였다. 그녀는 1415년 영국 스태퍼드실르에서 John Blount III(와)과 결혼했다. 그들은 적어도 열 명 이상의 아들과 여덟 명의 딸을 두었다. 그녀는 1440년 10월 28일 영국 잉글랜드 슈럽실르 킨렛에서 46세의 나이로 사망했다.
